title: Turbinicarpus mandragora
taxonomy: Turbinicarpus mandragora is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Turbinicarpus and is closely related to other species within this genus. Turbinicarpus mandragora is further classified as being part of the subgenus Pediocactus and the section Turbinicarpus. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and growth habits. Turbinicarpus mandragora is a unique species within the Cactaceae family.

intro: Turbinicarpus mandragora, also known as the "Mandragora Ball Cactus," is a rare and endangered species of cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its globular shape and sharp, protective spines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The bioactive constituents of Turbinicarpus mandragora include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Turbinicarpus mandragora is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and gastrointestinal issues.
